feedback.text: Please ensure video games are explicitly included. Many titles sold as one-time purchases rely on online servers for DRM or core functions. When those servers are shut down, the product brickseven for single-player useso consumers lose access to something they paid for and valuable digital heritage disappears. Add a sunset safeguard: once official support ends, publishers must release an offline patch or the minimal server code needed to keep the game playable, and prohibit DRM that disables titles after shutdown. This simple change aligns with the Acts goal of stopping unfair digital practices, safeguards millions of legitimate buyers, and preserves Europes creative industry. Thank you for considering this adjustment.
